summary: This course is designed primarily around the key question of 'Whose rules do we use when we interact with people from other cultures?' We will consider this question in relation to intercultural pragmatics and pragmalinguistics, speech functions in language learning, English as a Lingua Franca, Intercultural Communicative Competence, Willingness to Communicate (WTC), and educational culture.
aims:
1. Analyze and compare Japanese culture with North American culture at an advanced level.
2. Develop critical awareness of communicative patterns used in intercultural communication at an advanced level.
3. Express spoken and written understanding the themes raised in the course.
goals:
Examine intercultural pragmatics and pragmalinguistics
Focus on speech functions in language learning
Develop Willingness to Communicate in English
Compare communicative culture at a high level

plan.text:
Course Introduction: Whose rules do we use when we interact with people from other cultures?
Expectations of student work and a detailed assessment philosophy will be outlined.
The Developmental Model of Intercultural Sensitivity (DMIC)
Language Education and Culture I: Task-based Language Teaching (TBLT)
Language Education and Culture II: English as a Lingua Franca (ELF)
Language Education and Culture III: Immersion education and Content-based Language Teaching (CBLT)
Course Reflection and Consolidation. Guidelines for final report

textbooks: All course materials and readings will be provided
prereq: Open to third-year students and above. This class will be taught in English and students will be expected to contribute using English only. Students will have successfully completed the introductory Intercultural Communication I course (or my Comparative Culture course) with a strong performance before taking this course. Prior consultation with the instructor is highly recommended before enrolling in this course. Students will be required to prepare for this course by reading one academic article each week. There will be weekly tests based on these readings to begin each class, followed by a discussion of the assigned reading. Class size will be limited to 12 students.
prep: Students will be expected to read academic articles in English every week in preparation for weekly tests and class discussions. This is a high level course with a basic teacher expectation that students will do all of the required weekly work (this is will be extensive), and be ready to actively discuss the weekly content.
teachers: ◎ ダレン リングリー

extra.text: Class size will be limited to 12 students, based on English ability and performance in prerequisite courses (IC Communication I). Preference will be given to students who are doing teacher licensing, students who have performed well in my intro/survey courses in Intercultural Communication/Comparative Culture, and students who have completed study abroad programs.
